title: Use regime-specific experts with rolling-origin validation and OOF stacking
summary: The literature repeatedly points to compound-specific and context-dependent degradation, while scikit-learn notes grouped stratification is only approximate.
rationale: The literature repeatedly points to compound-specific and context-dependent degradation, while scikit-learn notes grouped stratification is only approximate. A single global model may blur dry/wet, first-stop/late-stop, and early/late-race behaviors.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for regimes like dry vs wet, stint 1 vs later stints, and early/mid/late race; generate OOF predictions under stricter splits such as leave-one-race-out plus a year-blocked or rolling-origin sanity check; then stack with a tiny meta-learner using regime indicators.
expected_effect: Better specialization and a more trustworthy CV estimate; can also reveal whether current 0.935 CV is optimistic under temporal shift.
risk: Smaller regime subsets may overfit, and stricter validation may reduce local CV even if leaderboard generalization improves.
